name: A167 DURHAM WESTERN BYPASS 1/19A
units: metres below ground level; typed site-investigation log, description transcribed verbatim

intervals.verbatim: TOPSOIL
Stiff slightly laminated mottled orange brown and grey silty CLAY with occasional fine and medium subangular and subrounded gravel

Very stiff with occasional shear surfaces brown with some bands and pockets of grey silty CLAY and occasional fine and medium angular gravel of coal:band of laminated brown silty clay encountered below 3.00m
annotations: coal present only as occasional angular detrital gravel within the clay, not a seam

intervals.verbatim: Stiff laminated brown silty CLAY with occasional fine and medium subangular and subrounded gravel and some partings of brown silt:many bands of grey slightly sandy silty clay with fine to coarse subangular and subrounded gravel
Medium dense yellow brown silty fine SAND:becoming slightly clayey with depth
Dense orange brown clayey sandy SILT
Medium dense to dense brown with some pockets of yellow brown clayey silty fine and medium SAND
annotations: bed spans the Sheet 1/Sheet 2 boundary (continues on Sheet 2)

intervals.verbatim: Medium dense to dense brown clayey silty fine and medium SAND with some bands of brown sandy silt,yellow brown silty fine sand and brown silty clay

Medium dense to dense yellow brown becoming orange brown clayey sandy SILT with some bands of orange brown and pale brown clayey sandy silt, brown silty clay and occasional pockets of black carbonaceous fine sand
annotations: black carbonaceous fine sand is dispersed carbon, not a coal seam

intervals.verbatim: Medium dense to dense orange brown becoming pale brown clayey sandy SILT with many bands of brown slightly clayey silty fine sand,occasional pockets of black carbonaceous fine sand and some bands of laminated brown silty clay
annotations: GWs (groundwater strike/seepage) logged at 19.10m in samples column

intervals.verbatim: Laminated brown silty CLAY with occasional partings of silty fine sand
Medium dense pale brown clayey silty fine SAND with occasional bands of laminated brown clayey silt
Medium dense brown sandy SILT with occasional bands of brown silty clay
annotations: bed spans the Sheet 2/Sheet 3 boundary (top of Sheet 3)
GWe (groundwater) logged near 20m in water column

intervals.verbatim: Firm laminated brown silty CLAY with some partings of brown and grey silt
Dense brown clayey sandy SILT with occasional pockets of black carbonaceous fine sand and bands of brown silty clay

Medium dense to dense brown clayey silty fine to coarse SAND with occasional partings of silty fine sand, pockets of black carbonaceous fine sand and many bands of laminated brown silty clay and brown clayey sandy silt
annotations: GWs (groundwater strike/seepage) logged at 23.50m in samples column

intervals.verbatim: Firm to stiff and stiff laminated brown silty CLAY with some partings of brown sandy silt and many bands of brown slightly sandy silty clay with some fine to coarse subangular and subrounded gravel
Brown clayey silty fine and medium SAND with some fine to coarse subangular and subrounded gravel and occasional pockets of orange brown fine and medium sand

Firm greyish brown very sandy silty CLAY with occasional fine and medium subangular and subrounded gravel and occasional cobbles,some pockets of pale brown silty fine sand and occasional bands of laminated brown silty clay with some partings of brown fine sand at 30.30m
annotations: bed spans the Sheet 3/Sheet 4 boundary (continues on Sheet 4)

intervals.verbatim: Yellow brown silty fine and medium SAND

Stiff laminated brown silty CLAY with some partings of brown silt and brown silty fine sand and many pockets and bands of brown very sandy silty clay with occasional fine to coarse subangular and subrounded gravel
annotations: GWs (groundwater strike/seepage) logged at 32.40m in samples column

intervals.verbatim: Dense brown slightly clayey silty fine and medium SAND with some pockets of brown silty clay
Stiff greyish brown sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular to subrounded gravel
annotations: END OF BOREHOLE at 36.50m; hole bottomed in drift, no rockhead reached

notes: Modern typed Wimpey Geotech cable-percussion log (Sheets 1-4 of 4), A167 Durham Western Bypass Supplementary Ground Investigation, Fig 21; originator DJM, checked LKR, dated 15/6/92; Lab Ref S/26671; commenced 8/6/92. Ground level 89.00 m OD. ENTIRE sequence is Quaternary drift (glacial till / glaciolacustrine silty clays and silts / glaciofluvial clayey sands) — no rockhead (Coal Measures bedrock) reached; hole bottomed at 36.50 m bgl (52.50 m OD) in stiff sandy silty CLAY. All 23 beds classed 'drift': the log names soils (CLAY / SAND / SILT with density-consistency terms), never SANDSTONE/MUDSTONE/SILTSTONE rock. Reduced levels in the OD Level column cross-check EXACTLY against the 89.00 datum minus cumulative depth for every boundary (e.g. 2.00m=87.00, 15.50m=73.50, 36.50m=52.50); NO clerk arithmetic slips found. No named coal seams: 'coal' appears only as detrital angular gravel in the 2.00-3.50m clay, and 'black carbonaceous fine sand' pockets are dispersed carbon. Remarks box (all sheets): inspection pit to 1.20m; ground-water encountered overnight 9-10/6, level rose to 19.10m; lockable steel cover concreted over piezometer installation. Water added during boring 6.00-19.00m and 20.00-27.00m; chiselling 27.20-27.80m for 1.00h. GW strike/seepage markers logged at 19.10, ~20, 23.50 and 32.40m (recorded on nearest intervals). Note: index depth_m was 38.5 but the actual final depth on the log is 36.50m. Pages 5-8 are location/site plans (Site Plan 1 by Roughton & Partners for DTp, plus general layout sheets) with NO strata; read but not transcribed. Scan legible throughout; all intervals confidence=clear.
